content:
First, by similarity of ΔABC and ΔACD we have the correspondence

 A↔A, B↔C, C↔D.

Hence the similarity‐ratio is k=AC/AB=6/4=3/2, and so  
 CD=k·BC=(3/2)·5=7.5,  
 DA=k·CA=(3/2)·6=9.  

We now place  
 A=(0,0),  C=(6,0),  
and solve for D=(x,y) with  
 |AD|=9,  |CD|=7.5.  
One finds  
 D=(81/16,\;−45√7/16)  (taking the sign so that ABCD is convex).  

Next P lies on the ray DA beyond A, so P=A+t(A−D), t>0.  
Thus  
 P=(−(81/16)t,\;(45√7/16)t).  

The condition ∠BDC=∠ACP is equivalent to  
 cos∠BDC = cos∠ACP.  
A direct dot–product calculation gives  
 cos∠BDC = 33/(4√79),  
and  
 cos∠ACP = (81t+96)/(144t+96).  

Setting  
 (81t+96)/(144t+96) = 33/(4√79)  
and solving for t yields  
 t = (88√79 −576)/1575.  

One then checks easily that  
 DP = 9(1+t)  
and so  
 DP = 9·(88√79+999)/(1575) = (88√79+999)/175.  

Hence  
 DP² = [(88√79+999)/175]² = ( 999+88√79 )²/30625.  

Final answer:  
\[
\boxed{\frac{(\,999+88\sqrt{79}\,)^{2}}{30625}}.
\]

 D=(81/16,\;−45√7/16)  (taking the sign so that ABCD is convex).  

Next P lies on the ray DA beyond A, so P=A+t(A−D), t>0.

 P=(−(81/16)t,\;(45√7/16)t).

 cos∠BDC = cos∠ACP.

 cos∠ACP = (81t+96)/(144t+96).

\]
